entities.name: semaglutide
entities.context: Anecdotal experience raised the possibility that semaglutide, a glucagon-like peptide 1 receptor agonist (GLP-1 RA) with rapidly increasing use, is associated with nonarteritic anterior ischemic optic neuropathy (NAION).

entities.name: GLP-1 receptor agonism
entities.context: semaglutide, a glucagon-like peptide 1 receptor agonist (GLP-1 RA) with rapidly increasing use

entities.name: Nonarteritic Anterior Ischemic Optic Neuropathy
entities.context: Anecdotal experience raised the possibility that semaglutide, a glucagon-like peptide 1 receptor agonist (GLP-1 RA) with rapidly increasing use, is associated with nonarteritic anterior ischemic optic neuropathy (NAION).

entities.name: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
entities.context: Propensity matching was used to assess whether prescribed semaglutide was associated with NAION in patients with type 2 diabetes (T2D) or overweight/obesity

entities.name: Overweight and Obesity
entities.context: Propensity matching was used to assess whether prescribed semaglutide was associated with NAION in patients with type 2 diabetes (T2D) or overweight/obesity

entities.name: Systemic Hypertension
entities.context: accounting for covarying factors (sex, age, systemic hypertension, T2D, obstructive sleep apnea, obesity, hyperlipidemia, and coronary artery disease)

entities.name: Obstructive Sleep Apnea
entities.context: accounting for covarying factors (sex, age, systemic hypertension, T2D, obstructive sleep apnea, obesity, hyperlipidemia, and coronary artery disease)

entities.name: Hyperlipidemia
entities.context: accounting for covarying factors (sex, age, systemic hypertension, T2D, obstructive sleep apnea, obesity, hyperlipidemia, and coronary artery disease)

entities.name: Coronary Artery Disease
entities.context: accounting for covarying factors (sex, age, systemic hypertension, T2D, obstructive sleep apnea, obesity, hyperlipidemia, and coronary artery disease)

entities.name: NAION incidence in T2D patients (semaglutide vs non-GLP-1 RA)
entities.context: A Cox proportional hazards regression model showed higher risk of NAION for patients receiving semaglutide (hazard ratio [HR], 4.28; 95% CI, 1.62-11.29); P < .001).

entities.name: NAION incidence in overweight/obese patients (semaglutide vs non-GLP-1 RA)
entities.context: A Cox proportional hazards regression model showed a higher risk of NAION for patients prescribed semaglutide (HR, 7.64; 95% CI, 2.21-26.36; P < .001).

entities.name: Ischemic Optic Neuropathy Risk with Semaglutide Retrospective Cohort Study

attributes.primary_endpoint: cumulative incidence and hazard ratio of NAION
attributes.patient_population: patients with type 2 diabetes or overweight/obesity evaluated by neuro-ophthalmologists
attributes.statistical_method: Kaplan-Meier and Cox proportional hazards regression

entities.context: In a retrospective matched cohort study using data from a centralized data registry of patients evaluated by neuro-ophthalmologists at 1 academic institution from December 1, 2017, through November 30, 2023

relations.evidence: semaglutide, a glucagon-like peptide 1 receptor agonist (GLP-1 RA) with rapidly increasing use
Prescriptions for semaglutide vs non-GLP-1 RA medications to manage either T2D or weight.
Prescriptions for semaglutide vs non-GLP-1 RA medications to manage either T2D or weight.
This study's findings suggest an association between semaglutide and NAION. As this was an observational study, future study is required to assess causality.

relations.evidence: In a retrospective matched cohort study using data from a centralized data registry of patients evaluated by neuro-ophthalmologists at 1 academic institution from December 1, 2017, through November 30, 2023

relations.evidence: Propensity matching was used to assess whether prescribed semaglutide was associated with NAION in patients with type 2 diabetes (T2D) or overweight/obesity, in each case accounting for covarying factors
accounting for covarying factors (sex, age, systemic hypertension, T2D, obstructive sleep apnea, obesity, hyperlipidemia, and coronary artery disease) and contraindications for use of semaglutide
